title: Sophia's Bedtime Story Hour (Rated: Surprisingly Violent)

sentences.en: Sunday night. William refused to sleep unless Sophia told him "the real one," which means the old folk tale about the castle on the hill.
sentences.zh: 星期天晚上。William 說什麼都不肯睡，除非 Sophia 講「那個真正的故事」，也就是那個關於山丘上城堡的古老民間故事。

sentences.en: I have heard this tale about a dozen times, but I sat on the floor anyway, because honestly, Sophia tells it differently every single time.
sentences.zh: 這個故事我已經聽過十幾次了，但我還是坐在地板上聽，因為老實說，Sophia 每次講的版本都不太一樣。

sentences.en: "A long time ago, many centuries before either of you were born," she began, "there was a kingdom ruled by an old king with a long white beard."
sentences.zh: 「很久很久以前，好幾個世紀以前，你們倆都還沒出生，」她開始說：「有一個王國，統治者是一位留著長長白鬍子的老國王。」

sentences.en: "The king had one daughter, a princess who was braver than every soldier in his royal army combined."
sentences.zh: 「國王有一位女兒，一位比他皇家軍隊裡每一位士兵加起來都還勇敢的公主。」

sentences.en: "The queen had died many years before, so the princess grew up learning both royal manners and how to use a sword."
sentences.zh: 「王后早在許多年前就過世了，所以這位公主從小同時學會了皇家禮儀，以及怎麼使劍。」

sentences.en: William interrupted immediately. "Where is the dragon? You promised a dragon!"
sentences.zh: William 馬上打斷：「龍呢？你說過會有龍的！」

sentences.en: "Patience," Sophia said. "Every good tale takes its time, just like every good soup."
sentences.zh: 「耐心點，」Sophia 說：「每個好故事都需要時間醞釀，就跟每一碗好湯一樣。」

sentences.en: "Beyond the mountains lived an enemy kingdom, and between the two kingdoms sat an old evil dragon who guarded a mountain of treasure: gold, silver, and diamonds."
sentences.zh: 「群山之外住著一個敵對的王國，而兩個王國之間，住著一隻邪惡的老龍，守著一整座黃金、白銀和鑽石堆成的寶藏山。」

sentences.en: "The enemy king wanted the treasure for himself, and he was ready to start a war to take it."
sentences.zh: 「敵國的國王想要獨吞那座寶藏，他準備發動戰爭來奪取它。」

sentences.en: "But the princess believed war was not the only path to victory. She believed in justice, and she believed there was always another way."
sentences.zh: 「但公主相信，戰爭並不是通往勝利唯一的道路。她相信正義，也相信一定還有別的辦法。」

sentences.en: "So instead of leading her army into battle, the princess climbed the mountain alone, carrying only a lantern and a small offering of bread."
sentences.zh: 「所以公主沒有帶著軍隊上戰場，反而獨自一人爬上那座山，只帶著一盞燈籠和一小份麵包當作獻禮。」

sentences.en: "She found the dragon curled around his treasure, and instead of attacking him, she simply sat down and offered him the bread."
sentences.zh: 「她找到那隻盤踞在寶藏上的龍，卻沒有攻擊牠，只是坐了下來，把麵包遞給牠。」

sentences.en: "'Nobody has ever asked what I wanted,' the dragon said, surprised. 'Everyone only wants my treasure. They never ask about my fate.'"
sentences.zh: 「『從來沒有人問過我想要什麼，』龍驚訝地說：『大家只想要我的寶藏，從來沒有人關心過我的命運。』」

sentences.en: "It turned out the dragon was terribly lonely, guarding gold and silver he could not even eat, forgotten by the whole world for a hundred years."
sentences.zh: 「原來這隻龍非常孤單，牠守著吃不下肚的黃金白銀，被整個世界遺忘了一百年。」

sentences.en: "The princess promised the dragon a home in her own royal garden, where servants would visit him and remember his name."
sentences.zh: 「公主答應要在自己皇家的花園裡給龍一個家，讓僕人們會去探望他，記得他的名字。」

sentences.en: "In exchange, the dragon agreed to guard the border in peace instead of guarding a pile of gold in silence."
sentences.zh: 「作為交換，龍答應要平靜地守護邊境，而不是安靜地守著一堆黃金。」

sentences.en: "When the enemy king arrived expecting a battle, he found the princess, the dragon, and a peaceful border instead of a war."
sentences.zh: 「當敵國國王抵達，準備打一場仗的時候，他看到的卻是公主、龍，還有一片和平的邊境，而不是戰爭。」

sentences.en: "Even the old temple priest, who had predicted war for years, admitted he had never seen a stranger or better kind of victory."
sentences.zh: 「就連多年來一直預言會爆發戰爭的老祭司，都承認他從沒見過這麼奇特、這麼美好的勝利方式。」

sentences.en: "And that," Sophia said, closing the story, "is why our family always says treasure means nothing if nobody remembers your name."
sentences.zh: 「而這，」Sophia 說完故事收尾：「就是為什麼我們家一直說，如果沒有人記得你的名字，寶藏根本什麼都不是。」

sentences.en: William, already half asleep, mumbled that he still wished there had been at least one proper sword fight.
sentences.zh: William 已經半睡半醒，嘴裡還喃喃說著他還是希望至少有一場正式的劍鬥場面。

sentences.en: I told him some of the best stories in the world do not need a fight to have a victory in them.
sentences.zh: 我跟他說，世界上有些最棒的故事，根本不需要打鬥也能有一場勝利。

sentences.en: Sophia turned off the light and said this tale was older than this house, this street, maybe even this whole town.
sentences.zh: Sophia 關了燈，說這個故事比這棟房子、這條街，甚至比整個小鎮都還要古老。

sentences.en: I do not know if a lonely treasure dragon ever really existed, but I like believing some old folk tales carry a little bit of truth inside them.
sentences.zh: 我不知道那隻孤單守寶藏的龍是不是真的存在過，但我喜歡相信，有些古老的民間故事裡，多少藏著一點真實。

sentences.en: Lesson of the day: sometimes the bravest thing a princess, or anyone, can do is offer bread instead of a sword.
sentences.zh: 今日心得：有時候一位公主，或任何人，能做最勇敢的事，就是遞出一塊麵包，而不是一把劍。

quiz.question: What does the princess offer the dragon instead of attacking him?
options: A lantern and a small offering of bread.

quiz.question: Why was the dragon so lonely?
options: He guarded treasure he could not eat, forgotten by everyone for a hundred years.
He was too fierce for anyone to visit.
He lived too far from the kingdom.
He had been banished by the king.

quiz.question: What does the family say treasure means nothing without?
